content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Most Angular developers learn RxJS by memorizing operators.
switchMap()
mergeMap()
concatMap()
exhaustMap()
We memorize what each one does and hope we’ll remember the right one during an interview.
That’s exactly how I approached one of my interviews.
Then the interviewer asked a simple question that completely changed my perspective.
The Interview
The interviewer asked:
“A user clicks the same button multiple times. How would you handle it?”
I immediately started explaining RxJS operators.
“We can use switchMap…”
“Or maybe exhaustMap…”
The interviewer interrupted me.
“I’m not asking which operator you know. I’m asking how you’d solve the business problem.”
That sentence completely changed how I think about RxJS.
The operator isn’t the solution.
The business requirement is.
